accordion: Folds and unfolds on command. The only obedient one here.
alert-dialog: Stops you before you do the thing. Someone has to.
alert: Yells politely. Usually about something you ignored.
aspect-ratio: Keeps media shaped while the layout does jazz hands.
avatar-group: A tiny pile of people. Blame distribution included.
avatar: A face circle for users, bots, and suspicious initials.
badge: A small loud sticker with a job.
banner: A page-wide shout without becoming a hostage note.
breadcrumb: Leaves a trail out. You will want it.
button-group: A button family. Fewer crumbs on the floor.
calendar: Knows what day it is, unlike the marquee.
callout: Interrupts politely. Refuses to be invisible.
card: A box that lifts when touched. Do not feed after midnight.
carousel: Slides sideways because forward was too normal.
chart: Turns numbers into loud little bars.
checkbox: Checked or unchecked. No feelings about it either way.
code-block: Code in a box. The box has opinions.
combobox: A dropdown that went to grad school.
command: ⌘K and it fetches. Good palette. Best palette.
context-menu: Right-click summons it. Mind what you summon.
copy-button: Copies text before users invent a new bug.
data-table: Sorts, filters, paginates, selects rows, holds grudges.
dialog: Traps focus like it is paid to. Radix money.
dropdown-menu: Opens downward, keyboard-navigable. Unhinged, not rude.
field: Label, help, error. A form with adult supervision.
form: Groups inputs before they form a union.
hover-card: Lurks until you hover. Then it is charming.
image-card: Media with a frame and a small ego.
marquee: Scrolls forever. We stopped asking why.
menubar: Menus for apps that own a blazer.
navigation-menu: Big navigation with tiny hallway energy.
ornaments: The sticker sheet escaped containment, so we made it a product.
otp-input: Six boxes. One tiny security ritual.
pagination: Turns one long list into supervised chunks.
popover: Pops over. Escape sends it home.
radio: One choice survives. The others cope.
resizable: Panels you can drag until someone complains.
separator: A line. It separates. We pay it anyway.
sheet: A side panel entering with opinions.
skeleton: It is not loading. It is molting.
sonner: Tiny interface pigeons that arrive, yell, and leave.
spinner: Rotates so you do not have to.
stepper: One step at a time, like the therapist said.
switch: Flips. Commits. No takebacks until you flip it back.
tabs: Multiple personalities, one panel at a time.
theme-selector: Changes outfits without opening the lab.
timeline: Events in order. Time stays linear. Nothing else does.
toggle-group: Tiny switches travelling in a pack.
